channel.title: NPR Topics: News
channel.description: NPR news, audio, and podcasts. Coverage of breaking stories, national and world news, politics, business, science, technology, and extended coverage of major national and world events.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: At the 2026 Met Gala, 'Fashion is Art.' Here's what to expect
item.description: On the first Monday of every May, the rich, famous and well-dressed gather in New York City for the Met Gala fundraiser. Ivy Buck offers a lookahead - covering this year's theme, where to watch, who's coming, and more.
item.content:encoded:
On the first Monday of every May, the rich, famous and well-dressed gather in New York City for the Met Gala fundraiser. Ivy Buck offers a lookahead - covering this year's theme, where to watch, who's coming, and more.

(Image credit: Savion Washington/Getty Images)
item.dc:creator: Ivy Buck

item.title: Trump administration falls behind on wildfire prevention with risky fire season ahead
item.description: Many of the nation's overgrown forests are at high risk of burning. Under the Trump administration, work to reduce flammable vegetation fell by more than a million acres compared to previous years.
item.content:encoded:
Many of the nation's overgrown forests are at high risk of burning. Under the Trump administration, work to reduce flammable vegetation fell by more than a million acres compared to previous years.

(Image credit: Justin Sullivan/Getty Images)
item.dc:creator: Lauren Sommer

item.title: In many states, election-denying candidates are running to control voting
item.description: In 23 states, including five presidential swing states, candidates who have denied election results are running for offices that have a direct role in certifying future elections, a new report finds.
item.content:encoded:
In 23 states, including five presidential swing states, candidates who have denied election results are running for offices that have a direct role in certifying future elections, a new report finds.

(Image credit: Ross D. Franklin)
item.dc:creator: Miles Parks

item.title: The oldest millennials are 45! This tool helps plan for longevity
item.description: The oldest millennials are turning 45 this year. The oldest Gen Xers are now 60. So how prepared will they be to thrive in the decades ahead? A new tool helps people assess beyond just savings.
item.content:encoded:
The oldest millennials are turning 45 this year. The oldest Gen Xers are now 60. So how prepared will they be to thrive in the decades ahead? A new tool helps people assess beyond just savings.

(Image credit: Mar Hernández for NPR)
item.dc:creator: Allison Aubrey

item.title: After NPR investigation, new bill aims to stop 'claim sharks' targeting disabled vets
item.description: The bipartisan bill would block companies from using auto-dialers to harvest private information about disabled veterans. The firms charge vets for a service lawmakers say is supposed to be free.
item.content:encoded: The bipartisan bill would block companies from using auto-dialers to harvest private information about disabled veterans. The firms charge vets for a service lawmakers say is supposed to be free.
item.dc:creator: Caley Fox Shannon

item.title: After Republicans blocked Indiana redistricting, millions poured in to defeat them
item.description: President Trump has thrown his support behind challengers to Republican state senators who opposed his redistricting push.
item.content:encoded:
President Trump has thrown his support behind challengers to Republican state senators who opposed his redistricting push.

(Image credit: Tamara Keith)
item.dc:creator: Tamara Keith

item.title: 2 US service members missing after military exercises in Morocco
item.description: Two U.S. service members are missing in southwestern Morocco after taking part in annual multinational military exercises in the North African country, the United States Africa Command (AFRICOM) said.
item.content:encoded:
Two U.S. service members are missing in southwestern Morocco after taking part in annual multinational military exercises in the North African country, the United States Africa Command (AFRICOM) said.

(Image credit: Mosa'ab Elshamy)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Trump says the U.S will 'guide' stranded ships from the Strait of Hormuz
item.description: The United States will launch an effort on Monday to "guide" stranded ships from the Iran-gripped Strait of Hormuz, President Donald Trump said, as two ships around the strait reported attacks.
item.content:encoded:
The United States will launch an effort on Monday to "guide" stranded ships from the Iran-gripped Strait of Hormuz, President Donald Trump said, as two ships around the strait reported attacks.

(Image credit: Razieh Poudat/AP)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Hantavirus outbreak kills 3 on cruise ship in the Atlantic Ocean, WHO says
item.description: Hantaviruses are usually spread by exposure to urine, saliva or feces from infected rodents, such as rats or mice. Hantavirus infections are rare but can cause deadly respiratory infections.
item.content:encoded:
Hantaviruses are usually spread by exposure to urine, saliva or feces from infected rodents, such as rats or mice. Hantavirus infections are rare but can cause deadly respiratory infections.

(Image credit: AFP via Getty Images)
item.dc:creator: Rob Stein

item.title: Gas prices went up more than 30 cents a gallon last week. How high could they go?
item.description: U.S. gas prices were nearly $3 an average prior to the start of the war in Iran.
item.content:encoded:
U.S. gas prices were nearly $3 an average prior to the start of the war in Iran.

(Image credit: Jenny Kane)
item.dc:creator: Chandelis Duster
